source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In a tournament each of the six teams plays one match against every other team. In each round of matches, three take place simultaneously. A TV station has already decided which match it will broadcast for each round, as shown in the diagram. In which round will team $\mathrm{S}$ play against team U?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the TV broadcast choices are 'shown in the diagram'
the asked match round cannot be determined from the text alone because the decisive schedule information is referenced as visual content
multi_step: the task appears to require combining round-robin tournament constraints with the diagrammed broadcast schedule
solving likely involves identifying or reconstructing match assignments across rounds, then locating when team S plays team U
this is more than direct visual lookup because the question asks for an inferred round rather than simply reading a labeled value
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value ('1')
the target output format is clear: a round number
given the diagram and tournament constraints, a solver or checker could verify whether the identified round is consistent

decision_reasons: Asset registry is complete enough for recovery: question text, primary image, and numeric answer are all present.
The sample is highly image-dependent, but the required diagram asset exists, so the core semantics are available.
Initial scores indicate strong verifiability and solid overall usefulness, with no registry issues recorded.
Although the diagram is not fully transcribed in text, this is not a reject condition because the image is available.
